title: Requirements for Licensing under the 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs
content:
1. Certified copy of Articles of Incorporation or Partnership registered with SEC for corporations/partnerships, or Certificate of Registration with DTI for single proprietorships.
2. Proof of financial capacity:
   - For single proprietorship or partnership: verified income tax returns for past two years and savings account certificate with minimum balance of P500,000 plus authority to examine bank deposit.
   - For newly organized corporation: savings account certificate with minimum balance of P500,000 plus authority to examine.
   - For existing corporation: verified financial statements, corporate tax returns for past two years, and savings account certificate with minimum balance of P500,000 plus authority to examine.
3. Proof of marketing capability:
   a. Duly executed Special Power of Attorney and/or Recruitment/Service Agreement.
   b. Manpower requests or visa certifications from new employers/principals for at least 100 workers.
   c. Certification from POEA Pre-Employment Services Office on existence of new market.
4. Clearances from NBI and other government agencies for all board members, partners, or proprietors; foreign members require clearance from country of origin.
5. Verified undertaking stating that the applicant:
   a. Selects only medically and technically qualified recruits.
   b. Assumes full responsibility for all claims and liabilities related to license use.
   c. Assumes joint and solidary liability with employer for claims including wages, compensation, repatriation.
   d. Guarantees compliance with labor and social laws of Philippines and employment country.
   e. Assumes responsibility for acts of officials, employees, representatives in recruitment and placement.
   f. Negotiates best terms and conditions of employment.
   g. Discloses full terms and conditions of employment to workers.
   h. Deploys at least 100 workers to new markets within one year of license issuance.
   i. Provides orientation on recruitment procedures, terms, and relevant information to workers.
   j. Repatriates deployed workers and belongings when necessary.
6. For corporations or partnerships, verified undertaking by officers, directors, partners to be jointly and severally liable for claims arising from employer-employee relationship.
7. Contract of lease or proof of building ownership with office space of at least 100 square meters.
8. Individual income tax returns for past two years of proprietor, partners, stockholders/incorporators.
9. Proof that sole proprietor, partner, or CEO holds a bachelor's degree and has three years business experience.
10. List of all officials and personnel involved in recruitment and placement with appointments, bio-data, two passport-size photos, and clearances from NBI and Anti-Illegal Recruitment Branch.
11. Proof of publication of notice of application including names of proprietor, partners, incorporators, and officers.
12. Certificate of attendance of owner and/or CEO in a pre-application seminar conducted by the Administration.
